summary: High‑quality matrix mix captures a superb 6/11/76 show with airy sound and standout performances of Eyes of the World and St. Stephen > Dancin', though occasional channel‑swap glitches are reported.
review: The matrix treatment by Betty Board (Hunter Seamons) delivers an airy, soft‑forgiving mix that puts listeners right in the concert hall, and fans rave about the laid‑back 1976 vibe and a second set highlighted by a mesmerizing Eyes of the World and a funky St. Stephen > Dancin' transition. A handful of users report channel‑swapping or looping glitches, but the musical performance is overwhelmingly praised.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of detailed reviews (6 out of 8) give 5‑star praise for both the show and the matrix recording, with only two brief complaints about technical glitches; this yields a strong combined score weighted toward performance.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Airy, crisp mix that blends board and audience sources beautifully; soft, forgiving vocal treatment. Some listeners note channel‑swap issues and occasional song looping glitches.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set described as extraordinary with seamless transitions; overall vibe laid‑back and mellow, making it an above‑average 1976 performance.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia's tone described as mellow/quaalude‑smooth, fitting the relaxed 1976 atmosphere.
